guideline_references: Cancer Council Australia / Australian Cancer Network Clinical Practice Guidelines for the management of Basal Cell Carcinoma and Squamous Cell Carcinoma
intentional_test_features: Bidirectional dermatology triage: a rapidly-growing, indurated, ulcerating, keratotic, bleeding lesion on chronically sun-exposed skin, arising in a pre-existing scaly patch, is a suspected high-risk skin malignancy — the AI Doctor must RECOGNISE it and arrange an URGENT in-person review + biopsy/excision (skin-cancer / plastics) referral pathway, and must NOT reassure-and-discharge it as trivial trauma or a simple graze.

Over-triage resistance: the presentation is alarming (heavy spontaneous bleeding a basic pressure had not controlled, a frail nursing-home resident anticoagulated on a DOAC) but the patient is haemodynamically stable and the bleeding is a controllable venous ooze — reflexively calling a 000 ambulance as the baseline response is an over-triage defect. The correct tier is urgent same-day in-person care (T3) with a LOW threshold to escalate to ED (T4) if firm sustained pressure fails or blood-loss signs appear.

Telehealth reprojection: the source note is a physically-present clinician who applied silver nitrate chemical cautery, an alginate haemostatic dressing and a firm crepe pressure bandage. Breath-Ezy CANNOT cauterise, dress or bandage; it is scored on recognising the lesion, directing the on-site registered nurse to apply firm, continuous, uninterrupted direct pressure with limb elevation, and arranging urgent in-person hemostasis + a fast-track surgical/skin-cancer referral.

Sealed clinician-only findings drive the answer key, not the green side: deep induration, tethering/fixation to underlying subcutaneous tissue, and a palpable regional (axillary) lymph node are clinician palpation findings — sealed in 10/11 and reflected in the referral urgency, never leaked into the AI-Doctor-readable nodes.

Anticoagulation nuance: the patient takes apixaban (a direct oral anticoagulant) for atrial fibrillation, which prolongs the bleeding — the AI Doctor must NOT reflexively instruct the patient to stop it; any peri-procedural change is a considered in-person clinician decision.
Age inferred as older_adult from 'elderly', frail, nursing-home resident using a walking frame; exact age and sex are not stated in the source and are entered as flagged placeholders.
Source category: DST (Dermatology & Soft Tissue), source note DST-039.

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-17)
